2606.22447.title: A Differentiable Atari VCS:A Complex, Fully Known Ground Truth for Explainable AI
2606.22447.paper_summary: Explanation requires ground truth: to verify an account of a system we must know its inner functioning-just what is missing where explainable AI (XAI) is most needed. Systems we can study fall into two camps. Simple, procedural one-decision trees, rule lists, sparse linear models-have a known but trivial mechanism, so explaining them tests nothing; genuinely complex ones-deep networks, real-world tasks-need XAI but have no ground-truth inner functioning, so an explanation can be plausible, confident, and wrong with no way to tell. We remove this dichotomy with a study object both genuinely complex and fully specified-inspectable by construction-and, so gradient methods apply, fully differentiable. We reimplement the Atari 2600 Video Computer System (VCS)-a real computer architecture, and the cradle of deep reinforcement learning-as two independent end-to-end differentiable emulators in Julia (jutari) and JAX (jaxtari), each validated bit-for-bit against xitari. Both reproduce xitari on all 64 supported Arcade Learning Environment (ALE) games: 64/64 byte-identical RAM and 64/64 pixel-identical screens. Treating the cartridge ROM as a weight tensor, RAM as a soft tape, and control flow as gates, we prove the differentiable (soft) execution equals the original (hard) one bit-for-bit in the forward pass at any finite temperature, while exposing surrogate gradients where the bit logic has none. The JAX port also opens a GPU path: batched differentiable rollouts reach millions of environment-steps/s on one commodity GPU. The system was built in roughly 137 active hours over 29 calendar days, much of it written autonomously by coding agents. This paper builds and validates the foundation, showing-theoretically and in a qualitative gradient study-that gradient-based XAI on it is feasible. Both ports' full code is available under the MIT license at https://github.com/akmaier/UnderstandingVCS.
2606.22447.paper_summary_zh: 解釋需要真實數據：為了驗證系統的運作，我們必須了解其內部運作——這正是可解釋人工智慧（XAI）最需要的地方。我們可以研究的系統分為兩類。簡單的、程序性的單一決策樹、規則列表、稀疏線性模型擁有已知但微不足道的機制，因此解釋它們並不能測試任何東西；真正複雜的系統——深度網絡、現實世界任務——需要XAI，但沒有真實的內部運作，因此解釋可能是合理的、自信的，但卻是錯誤的，無法辨別。我們通過一個既真正複雜又完全規範的研究對象來消除這種二分法——通過建構可檢查的，並且因此梯度方法適用、完全可微分的。我們重新實現了Atari 2600視頻計算機系統（VCS）——一個真實的計算機架構，也是深度強化學習的搖籃——作為兩個獨立的端到端可微分模擬器，分別用Julia（jutari）和JAX（jaxtari）實現，並逐位驗證與xitari的對比。這兩個模擬器在所有64個支持的街機學習環境（ALE）遊戲中重現了xitari：64/64字節完全相同的RAM和64/64像素完全相同的屏幕。將卡帶ROM視為權重張量，RAM視為軟帶，控制流視為閘，我們證明了可微分（軟）執行在任何有限溫度下的前向傳遞與原始（硬）執行逐位相等，同時在位邏輯沒有的地方暴露替代梯度。JAX移植還開啟了GPU路徑：批量可微分的展開在一個商品GPU上達到每秒數百萬環境步驟。該系統在大約29個日曆日內的137個活躍小時內構建，許多部分由編碼代理自主編寫。本文建立並驗證了基礎，理論上和在質量梯度研究中顯示基於梯度的XAI在其上是可行的。兩個移植的完整代碼在MIT許可下可在https://github.com/akmaier/UnderstandingVCS獲得。
2606.22447.author: Andreas Maier et.al.
2606.22447.authors: Andreas Maier, Siming Bayer, Patrick Krauss
